questions.question: Why is magnesium oxide NOT recommended for anxiety or other neuropsychiatric purposes?

options.text: It is too expensive compared to other forms
It has only approximately 4% bioavailability, meaning 96% of what you swallow provides no systemic benefit
It causes dangerous spikes in blood magnesium levels
It is no longer available for purchase

questions.explanation: Magnesium oxide has only approximately 4% bioavailability, meaning you would need about 16 tablets to match the absorbed magnesium from a single therapeutic dose of glycinate. Despite being the most commonly sold and cheapest form, it is essentially ineffective for neuropsychiatric purposes. Its only appropriate use is as a laxative.

questions.question: What makes magnesium threonate unique compared to other magnesium forms?

options.text: It has the highest elemental magnesium content per capsule
It is the cheapest form available
It specifically crosses the blood-brain barrier and increases brain magnesium concentrations
It provides a strong laxative effect

questions.explanation: Magnesium threonate was specifically formulated to cross the blood-brain barrier and increase brain magnesium concentrations. The 2024 RCT showed that 12 weeks of supplementation produced significant improvements in executive function, working memory, and attention through enhanced synaptic plasticity.

questions.question: How does magnesium function as a natural NMDA receptor antagonist?

options.text: It permanently blocks all NMDA receptor activity
Magnesium ions sit in the NMDA receptor channel and block excessive calcium influx when the neuron is at resting potential, providing voltage-dependent neuroprotection
It destroys NMDA receptors over time
It converts NMDA receptors into GABA receptors

questions.explanation: Magnesium naturally sits in the NMDA receptor channel, blocking calcium influx when the neuron is at resting potential. This voltage-dependent block prevents excessive calcium entry and neuronal overexcitation when magnesium is adequate. This is the same pathway that ketamine targets as a rapid antidepressant — magnesium provides a gentler, physiological version.

questions.question: Which magnesium test accurately reflects body magnesium status, and why?

options.text: Serum magnesium, because it is the standard test ordered by most providers
Hair analysis, because it measures long-term magnesium intake
RBC (red blood cell) magnesium, because serum magnesium reflects only 1% of total body stores and can appear normal even with significant tissue depletion
Urine magnesium, because it shows how much is being excreted

questions.explanation: RBC magnesium measures intracellular stores and is far more accurate than serum magnesium, which reflects only 1% of total body magnesium. A 'normal' serum magnesium level does not rule out deficiency. The optimal RBC magnesium range is 5.0-6.5 mg/dL.

questions.question: Why is combining magnesium threonate and magnesium glycinate recommended for comprehensive neuropsychiatric benefit?

options.text: They are cheaper when purchased together
Threonate provides brain-specific benefit by crossing the blood-brain barrier, while glycinate provides systemic magnesium repletion and calming effects from glycine
One form cancels out the side effects of the other
It is only a marketing recommendation with no clinical basis

questions.explanation: Threonate specifically crosses the blood-brain barrier for cognitive/brain-specific benefit but provides only ~144mg elemental magnesium (insufficient for systemic repletion). Glycinate provides ~80% bioavailable systemic magnesium repletion plus calming effects from the glycine amino acid. Together, they address both cognitive and anxiolytic/systemic needs.

title: Magnesium Forms: Choosing the Right One for Your Brain - Quiz
